articles.headline: Benne Dosa Trends Across India as Social Media Sensation
articles.summary: The buttery Davangere-style benne dosa has evolved from a Karnataka staple into a national culinary trend. Popularized by eateries like Benne in Mumbai and Kokkam in Lucknow, the dish features a crisp, buttery texture made for social media. Owners report hundreds of daily orders as regional Indian cuisines gain popularity through digital discovery and hyper-local food formats.

articles.headline: CERC Deviation Rules Threaten Revenue of Clean Power Projects
articles.summary: The Central Electricity Regulatory Commission has set strict tolerance bands of 10% for wind and 5% for solar power deviations to ensure grid stability. Developers warn these performance rules could slash wind project revenues by 48% and solar by 11%. The industry fears the steep penalties for under- or over-production will eventually lead to higher consumer electricity tariffs.

articles.headline: Goa Celebrates Centenary of Iconic Artist Mario Miranda
articles.summary: Today marks the 100th birth anniversary of Mario Miranda, the legendary cartoonist whose work remains a cultural symbol of Goa and Mumbai. Miranda's intricate pen-and-ink sketches captured the essence of everyday life and local characters like Ms. Fonseca. Events include a dedicated exhibition at the Sunaparanta Goa Centre for the Arts and a state-organized centenary lecture series.

articles.headline: Action Thriller Series Man on Fire Set for Netflix Release
articles.summary: Yahya Abdul-Mateen II stars in a new Netflix action series based on A.J. Quinnell’s 1980 novel. The story follows a former US Special Forces mercenary pulled back into a life of violence for revenge. This adaptation follows previous screen versions, including the famous 2004 Denzel Washington film, promising a fresh take on the classic thriller narrative.

articles.headline: Artist Suman Dey Documents Time in Solo Exhibition at Emami Art
articles.summary: Kolkata’s Emami Art is hosting 'Chance Remains of Another Time,' a solo show by self-taught artist Suman Dey. The exhibition features abstract polyptych works that explore the passage of time through memories and natural forms. Dey’s unique language uses fragmented parts to construct a heterogeneous whole, documenting human interaction with physical and emotional spaces.

articles.headline: German Film Sound of Falling Explores Recurrent Themes Over Time
articles.summary: Mascha Schilinski’s acclaimed German film, 'Sound of Falling,' is now available on MUBI. Set on the same farm across four distinct time periods, the movie employs a dark, dreamlike progression. The narrative focuses on recurring gestures and themes over several years, providing a non-linear exploration of memory and location that resonated with critics globally last year.

articles.headline: Glory Thriller Series Explores Boxing Conspiracy in Haryana Academy
articles.summary: Netflix’s new Hindi thriller series 'Glory' features Suvinder Vicky, Divyenndu, and Pulkit Samrat. The plot centers on a star boxer's murder at a Haryana academy, dragging a coach and his estranged sons into a dangerous conspiracy. The series explores themes of corruption and family dynamics within the competitive world of Indian combat sports.

articles.headline: The Devil Wears Prada 2 Brings Original Cast Back to Theatres
articles.summary: A sequel to the modern classic 'The Devil Wears Prada' arrives in theatres two decades after the original. Meryl Streep returns as fearsome editor Miranda Priestly, alongside Anne Hathaway and Emily Blunt. The new cast includes Justin Theroux and Kenneth Branagh. The film follows the characters' evolved roles in the fashion world, written by original screenwriter Aline Brosh McKenna.

articles.headline: Pratibimb Marathi Natya Utsav Celebrates Evolving Theatre Traditions
articles.summary: Mumbai’s NCPA is hosting the Pratibimb Marathi Natya Utsav to showcase the evolving landscape of Marathi performing arts. The festival features mainstream and experimental plays, readings, and workshops by established and emerging practitioners. Highlights include a tribute to poet Bahinabai Chaudhary and writing labs aimed at nurturing new theatrical talent.

articles.headline: Michael Jackson Biopic Faces Criticism for Avoiding Serious Allegations
articles.summary: The new Hollywood musical biopic 'Michael' has broken international box office records but faces critical backlash for its portrayal of the pop star. Directed by Antoine Fuqua, the film is criticized as a 'soulless PR exercise' that depicts Jackson as a saint while omitting historical allegations of molestation. Critics argue the film lacks insight into the subject’s music and personal life.

articles.headline: Luxury Car Manufacturers Differentiate Through Sophisticated In-Car Technology
articles.summary: Mercedes-Benz, BMW, and Audi are rolling out flagship luxury vehicles for 2026 with near-identical technical specifications. Buyers now distinguish between the 'Big Three' based on passenger entertainment options and display screens. While basic features are now ubiquitous in sub-luxury cars, sophisticated tech packages remain the primary differentiator for high-end automobile purchases.

articles.headline: Stratford-upon-Avon Continues to Draw Global Shakespeare Tourists
articles.summary: The tradition of celebrating William Shakespeare's birth on April 23 continues to draw massive tourism to Stratford-upon-Avon. Modern pilgrimages to the playwright’s homes explore why the 18th-century jubilee tradition survived despite historical floods and logistical failures. The town remains an essential cultural destination, maintaining its status through rigorous heritage preservation.

articles.headline: AI Tools Force Employees to Rebuild Career Structures
articles.summary: LinkedIn's Aneesh Raman suggests that artificial intelligence is democratizing access to professional resources once reserved for those with elite connections. In his new book 'Open to Work,' Raman argues that organizations must rebuild structures around AI rather than tacking it on. Workers are encouraged to prioritize 'soft skills' and individual identity to remain relevant in the AI era.

articles.headline: J.G. Ballard’s The Drowned World Mirrors Modern Climate Anxieties
articles.summary: J.G. Ballard’s 1962 novel, 'The Drowned World,' is receiving renewed attention for its prophetic depiction of a planet ruined by solar radiation and rising temperatures. The story follows researchers in a submerged London, exploring the psychological regression of humanity in a primordial landscape. The book is cited as a haunting reflection of contemporary extreme heatwaves and climate flux.

articles.headline: Reliance on AI Assistants May Impede Social and Cognitive Skills
articles.summary: Psychotherapist Sonali Gupta warns that increasing reliance on AI for conversation and problem-solving may reduce our capacity to tolerate ambiguity and effort. Clients are increasingly treating AI as friends, potentially leading to social isolation. Gupta advocates for 'friction maxxing'—intentionally engaging in effortful real-world tasks to maintain cognitive functions and mental health.

articles.headline: Love Your Parks Mumbai Activates Green Spaces Through Community
articles.summary: Love Your Parks Mumbai (LYPMumbai) is transforming passive urban green spaces into active community hubs through nature walks, yoga, and jam sessions. Founded by Anca Florescu Abraham, the initiative aims to build emotional connections between citizens and public parks. Recent successes include mobilizing residents to protect trees and successfully resisting underground parking lot plans.

articles.headline: Fashion Labels Transform Wearable Garments into Sculptural Art
articles.summary: Designers including Gaurav Gupta, Iris Van Herpen, and Jean Paul Gaultier are blurring the lines between fashion and art. Notable pieces include Gupta’s ₹8 lakh Bordeaux Volute Corset Gown and Van Herpen’s laser-cut Sensory Seas Belt. These collections utilize pioneering techniques and anatomical prints to treat the human body as a canvas for high-concept sculptural expression.

articles.headline: Zach Galifianakis Hosts Wholesome Gardening Series on Netflix
articles.summary: Comedian Zach Galifianakis shifts from his abrasive 'Between Two Ferns' persona to host 'This Is A Gardening Show' on Netflix. The six-episode series features Galifianakis learning about agriculture from farmers and children. Critics describe the show as an 'oasis of calm' and a micro-dose of learning that prioritizes sunny optimism over sensationalism in the streaming landscape.

articles.headline: Voter List Purges and Citizenship Debates Spark National Controversy
articles.summary: Over 8 million voters were removed from electoral rolls in West Bengal following a Special Intensive Revision process, sparking allegations of political pruning. Nationally, 20.5 million names were removed in Uttar Pradesh. The development mirrors global shifts, such as Donald Trump's challenge to birthright citizenship in the US, as nations increasingly seek to redefine national identity.

articles.headline: Siddharth Kak Revisits Drama of India’s 1984 Everest Expedition
articles.summary: In his new book 'A Fire Over Mount Everest,' filmmaker Siddharth Kak provides a behind-the-scenes look at the 1984 mission that put Bachendri Pal on the summit. Kak details the internal frictions, command-style leadership, and the often-ignored contributions of female climbers. The memoir reflects on the life-threatening challenges of analogue filmmaking on the world's highest peak.

articles.headline: Workload Crisis in World Football Increases Player Injury Risks
articles.summary: Top footballers now face seasons with up to 80 matches, leading to a surge in major injuries for stars like Kylian Mbappé and Lamine Yamal. Players’ unions have filed legal cases against FIFA over expanded international calendars and the new Club World Cup. Experts warn the 'ruthless scheduling' for commercial gain is pushing players to physical breaking points ahead of the 2026 World Cup.

articles.headline: Indian Freedivers Set National Records and Prepare for World Championships
articles.summary: Athletes Akshay Thatte, Archana Thiagarajan, and Vachan Gowda are representing India in the global freediving scene. Thatte recently set a national record of 5 minutes 16 seconds in Static Apnea. The trio is preparing for the World Apnea Pool Championships in Hungary. The sport, which requires intense hypoxia tolerance, is growing in India as a form of underwater meditation and discipline.

articles.headline: Photography Legend Raghu Rai Passes Away at Age 83
articles.summary: Raghu Rai, the renowned photographer who captured the pulse of post-independence India, has died. A protégé of Henri Cartier-Bresson and a Padma Shri recipient, Rai documented historical moments including the Bhopal gas tragedy and the 1971 Bangladesh Liberation War. His extensive work spanned street photography and intimate portraits of figures like the Dalai Lama and Mother Teresa.

articles.headline: Soumya Sankar Bose Explores Memory and Death in Art Show
articles.summary: Artist Soumya Sankar Bose’s exhibition 'We Need to Talk In Whispers' at Kolkata's Experimenter is inspired by a journal found on a train. The project uses found photographs, text, and an offline AI bot to explore themes of thanatology and suicide notes. Bose creates a community archive of memories, asking how narratives are rebuilt after the original story-teller is gone.

articles.headline: Ticket to Kerala Book Provides Primer on Malayalam Cinema History
articles.summary: S.R. Praveen’s new book 'Ticket to Kerala' analyzes the global rise of Malayalam cinema in the OTT era. The work tracks the industry from J.C. Daniel’s early silent films to the modern wave led by Lijo Jose Pellissery. It also covers pivotal moments like the formation of the Women in Cinema Collective and the role of film societies in building the state's unique cinematic literacy.

articles.headline: Best-Seller Remarkably Bright Creatures Adapted for Screen With Sally Field
articles.summary: Shelby Van Pelt’s debut novel 'Remarkably Bright Creatures,' which sold 1.5 million copies, is being released as a film on Netflix on May 8. Starring Sally Field and featuring Alfred Molina as the voice of an intelligent giant Pacific octopus named Marcellus, the story explores grief, friendship, and mortality. The film retains the book’s central theme of an unlikely bond at a local aquarium.

articles.headline: Kabita Singha’s Feminist Classic Four Angry Women Translated Into English
articles.summary: Kabita Singha’s 1956 debut novel, 'Four Angry Women,' has been translated by Shamita Das Gupta. The modernist work follows four young women navigating 1950s Calcutta, challenging patriarchal decorum through their desire for freedom. Singha, a prominent Bengali poet, used the narrative to explore silent female rage and societal inequality, leaving a lasting legacy for feminist literature.

articles.headline: LPG and Aviation Fuel Prices Hike Sharply Amid Supply Constraints
articles.summary: State-run oil companies increased the price of commercial 19-kg LPG cylinders by ₹993 to ₹3,071.50 in Delhi on Friday. International aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices also rose by 5%, while bulk diesel prices climbed by ₹12 per litre. The hikes, driven by West Asia supply issues, are expected to increase costs for restaurants, hotels, and international air travel.

articles.headline: Centre Plans Reits for PSU Assets with Global Private Equity
articles.summary: The Indian government is exploring partnerships with global investors like Blackstone and Brookfield to form Real Estate Investment Trusts (Reits) for PSU assets. The plan involves monetizing optic fibre networks and land parcels from BSNL, RailTel, and PowerGrid. These assets could be repurposed for data centre infrastructure, unlocking capital for the government while retaining ownership.

articles.headline: Indus Towers Flags Supply Risks from West Asia Conflict
articles.summary: Indus Towers, a subsidiary of Bharti Airtel, has warned that disruptions in LPG supply from the West Asia conflict could slow tower production and delay network rollouts. LPG is a critical input in tower manufacturing used to heat steel for zinc coating. Any shortage threatens to increase costs and stall infrastructure development for telecom operators across the country.

articles.headline: Air India to Reduce International Flights Due to Jet Fuel Surge
articles.summary: Loss-making carrier Air India has announced plans to reduce international flight frequencies until July. The decision follows a surge in jet fuel prices and airspace restrictions in West Asia, which have made several routes unprofitable. Chief Executive Campbell Wilson stated the airline must adjust its schedule to manage rising operational costs and geopolitical flight path closures.

articles.headline: GIFT City IFSCA Sees Rising Interest in Corporate Treasury Centres
articles.summary: Six listed companies have applied for licenses to set up corporate treasury centres in GIFT City, according to IFSCA chairperson Kalyanaraman Rajaraman. The offshore financial hub allows MNCs to centralize global cash and risk management. Despite hurdles in cross-border cash automation, IFSCA expects authorizations to double or triple this year as more firms evaluate GIFT's tax incentives.

articles.headline: India Records Record GST Collection of ₹2.43 Trillion in April
articles.summary: Central and state governments collected an all-time high of ₹2.43 trillion in GST revenue in April. Even after adjusting for the phased-out compensation cess, gross collections grew 8.7% annually. The record revenue, achieved despite sharp tax rate cuts last September, indicates resilient consumption demand and strong year-end factory clearances across the Indian economy.

articles.headline: ISRO Targets Five Commercial SSLV Launches in Next Fiscal Year
articles.summary: ISRO plans to conduct five commercial launch missions for its Small Satellite Launch Vehicle (SSLV) in FY27. This follows the successful development of the rocket designed for low-earth orbits. With India's space industry generating $8.4 billion annually, the government aims to expand its global market share to 8% by 2033, focusing on cost-effective small satellite deployment.

articles.headline: Global Oil Giants Re-evaluate Investment in Venezuela Amid Price Surge
articles.summary: Major oil companies including ExxonMobil and ConocoPhillips are reconsidering operations in Venezuela as global oil prices reach $105 a barrel. Despite past legal disputes and nationalization, executives are assessment brownfields and abandoned fields. Recent changes to Venezuelan hydrocarbon laws and acting president Delcy Rodriguez's outreach have softened previous industry opposition.

articles.headline: Early Monsoon Forecast Offers Hope for Rural Consumption and Growth
articles.summary: The IMD expects the southwest monsoon to arrive early over the Andaman Islands by mid-May, predicting above-normal rainfall for the month. Early rains are critical for kharif sowing and could boost farm incomes, lifting rural demand. Economists estimate a favourable monsoon could add 20-40 basis points to India's FY27 GDP growth while helping to cool food inflation risks.

articles.headline: West Asia Market Selloff Dents Q4 Profits for Asset Managers
articles.summary: Listed asset management companies (AMCs) including ICICI Prudential and HDFC AMC saw sequential declines in net profit in Q4 FY26 due to mark-to-market losses. Geopolitical tensions in West Asia triggered a March stock market decline, eroding the value of AMCs' treasury portfolios. While management fee income remained stable, the sharp market correction impacted overall earnings for the quarter.

articles.headline: Burgundy Brand Collective Expands Luxury Retail Beyond Top Metros
articles.summary: Luxury retailer Burgundy Brand Collective aims to cross ₹200 crore in revenue by FY27, betting on premium consumption in newer Indian cities. The Mumbai-based firm, which recently acquired Le Mill, plans to double its store count to 65. The expansion targets affluent consumers spending across fashion, food, and home categories as India’s luxury market reaches a projected ₹5,000 crore.

articles.headline: Nine Arrested for Rs 37 Crore Student Aid Scheme Fraud
articles.summary: The Anti-Corruption Branch of Delhi arrested nine individuals, including directors of three coaching institutes, for allegedly siphoning Rs 37 crore from the Jai Bhim Mukhyamantri Pratibha Vikas Yojana. Between 2018 and 2021, the accused allegedly used fake student documents to claim government funds intended for educational aid for underprivileged students.

articles.headline: Delhi Private Schools Barred From Charging Multi-Month Fees in Advance
articles.summary: The Directorate of Education (DoE) has issued a directive stating that private schools in Delhi cannot compel parents to pay fees for more than one month at a time. The order follows numerous complaints about institutions forcing quarterly or advance payments. Schools are now mandated to accept monthly installments to ease the financial burden on guardians.

articles.headline: Punjab Registers First Case Under Controversial New Sacrilege Law
articles.summary: Police in Muktsar district registered the first FIR under the Jaagat Jyot Sri Guru Granth Sahib Satkar Act after torn pages of a prayer book were found in Malout town. The new law, which came into force on April 20, prescribes life imprisonment for sacrilege. Officials are scanning CCTV footage to determine if the incident was a deliberate act of mischief or accidental.

articles.headline: Seven-Year-Old Student Run Over by School Bus in Greater Noida
articles.summary: A seven-year-old girl died after being run over by her own school bus in the Dankaur area of Greater Noida. The driver reportedly completed his drop-off route before being arrested by the police. Authorities have initiated a probe into the safety protocols followed by the school transport department following the tragic incident.

articles.headline: IMD Predicts Milder May for India After Punishing April Heat
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecast that May will be cooler than usual across most of India due to increased western disturbances and thunderstorm activity. While some coastal states and Himalayan foothills may see extra heatwave days, most regions will experience normal to below-normal temperatures. Strengthening southwesterly winds suggest a timely monsoon arrival.

articles.headline: Punjab Opposition Demands Breathalyser Test for CM Bhagwant Mann
articles.summary: High drama ensued in the Punjab assembly as Congress and other opposition parties alleged Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann arrived in an inebriated state. Leader of Opposition Partap Singh Bajwa demanded a sobriety test for all members. Mann rejected the claims, accusing the opposition of lacking substantive issues to raise during the special session held to hike minimum wages.

articles.headline: Spirit Airlines Set to Cease Operations Amid Financial Crisis
articles.summary: Spirit Airlines is preparing to wind down operations as liquidity reaches critical lows following a surge in fuel costs. Talks with the U.S. government for rescue financing reportedly hit an impasse. The carrier, which had planned to exit bankruptcy by summer, has seen its financial position deteriorate significantly due to high energy prices linked to the West Asia conflict.

articles.headline: Elon Musk Testifies in High-Stakes OpenAI Breach of Promise Trial
articles.summary: Elon Musk spent three days testifying in a California court, accusing OpenAI of betraying its original non-profit mission. Musk alleges that the company’s $10 billion partnership with Microsoft violates its founding principles. OpenAI's lawyers countered by highlighting Musk's own for-profit AI ventures, while the judge noted the irony of Musk's claims regarding AI extinction risks.

articles.headline: SC Refuses to Block Abortion for 15-Year-Old Rape Victim
articles.summary: The Supreme Court dismissed a curative petition by AIIMS, allowing a 30-week pregnancy of a 15-year-old girl to be terminated. Chief Justice Surya Kant emphasized that reproductive autonomy and the girl's mental well-being take precedence over institutional concerns. The court ruled that the State cannot compel a minor to carry a fetus to term against her will.

articles.headline: Internal SC Quotas Won’t Apply to Completed Exams: Karnataka Govt
articles.summary: The Karnataka Cabinet clarified that its revised internal reservation formula for Scheduled Castes will only apply to future recruitment cycles where examinations have not yet been held. This decision protects ongoing hiring processes while operationalizing a 400-point roster system that allocates points across different SC sub-groups including Madiga and Holeya communities.

articles.headline: Andhra Pradesh Seeks Central Nod for Polavaram Project Phase-2
articles.summary: The Andhra Pradesh government is pushing the Centre for approval of the Polavaram project's second phase. Phase-I, nearing completion, aims to impound water to 41.15 metres, while Phase-II targets a level of 45.72 metres. Challenges remain regarding the rehabilitation of nearly 68,000 families and addressing submergence concerns raised by neighboring states like Odisha and Telangana.

articles.headline: Six Killed After High-Speed Car Rams Parked Truck Near Hyderabad
articles.summary: Six members of a family, including two children, died instantly when their car crashed into a truck parked on the Outer Ring Road near Shamshabad. The victims were returning to Hyderabad after visiting a temple. Police suspect the driver may have fallen asleep at the wheel of the vehicle, which was reportedly owned by the brother of a local BRS leader.

articles.headline: Centre Plans Rs 8,097 Crore Equity Infusion for Vizag Steel Plant
articles.summary: The Union government is set to announce a financial package of Rs 8,097 crore to revive the struggling Rashtriya Ispat Nigam Limited (RINL). The infusion will take the form of equity to provide operational relief. Trade unions have welcomed the move but continue to advocate for captive mines to reduce the shore-based plant’s high production costs and dependency on imports.

articles.headline: SC Calls for National SoP to Curb Use of Stock Witnesses
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has directed a committee to frame a nationwide Standard Operating Procedure to prevent the police from using 'stock witnesses' in criminal cases. The order follows a case where individuals were found appearing as witnesses in over 150 different investigations. The court described the practice as anathema to the rule of law and fair investigation.

articles.headline: Tigress and Cubs in Kanha Die Due to Canine Distemper Virus
articles.summary: Forest officials confirmed that a 10-year-old tigress and her four cubs died in Madhya Pradesh’s Kanha Tiger Reserve due to a Canine Distemper Virus (CDV) outbreak. Authorities have begun vaccinating local dogs and sanitizing forest water bodies to contain the infection. No symptoms have been detected in other tigers within the high-density habitat so far.

articles.headline: West Asia War Forces Global Shipping to Pivot Around Africa
articles.summary: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz and Red Sea tensions are redrawing global trade routes. Commercial vessel traffic around the Cape of Good Hope has tripled, while Red Sea transits fell by over 60%. Ports like Jeddah and Tanger Med are emerging as new logistics hubs as shipowners avoid the Bab al-Mandeb Strait. Rerouting has added two weeks to Asia-Europe transport times.

articles.headline: US Army Seeks to Deploy Dark Eagle Hypersonic Missile Against Iran
articles.summary: U.S. Central Command has requested the deployment of the 'Dark Eagle' hypersonic missile system to the Middle East. If approved, it would mark the first combat deployment of the long-delayed weapon, which can hit targets over 2,700km away at five times the speed of sound. The request follows a stalemate in peace negotiations and continued threats to maritime security in the region.

articles.headline: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg Blames Layoffs on High AI Capital Spending
articles.summary: Meta chief Mark Zuckerberg informed employees that upcoming layoffs, affecting roughly 10% of the workforce, are driven by a shift in capital allocation toward AI infrastructure. Zuckerberg stated the company must balance rising compute costs with personnel expenses. The social media giant is prioritizing long-term AI development despite the immediate impact on its global headcount.

articles.headline: Adani Group Announces Major Restructuring to Accelerate Growth Across Businesses
articles.summary: Billionaire Gautam Adani’s conglomerate revealed a plan to adopt a three-layer organizational structure to reduce decision-making time. The restructuring aims to push leaders closer to project sites and improve liquidity access. The group plans to invest $100 billion over the next six years, focusing on infrastructure, energy, and consumer sectors while scaling up market presence.

articles.headline: Arjun Erigaisi Holds Magnus Carlsen to Draw in Classical Chess Return
articles.summary: Indian Grandmaster Arjun Erigaisi played out a solid draw against world number one Magnus Carlsen in the opening round of the TePe Sigeman tournament in Sweden. Utilizing the Petroff Defense, Erigaisi maintained a stable position throughout the game, resulting in a threefold repetition. This marks Carlsen's first classical tournament appearance since June 2025.

articles.headline: Vikas Dhoot Appointed as New Editor of Business Standard
articles.summary: Vikas Dhoot (47) took over as the Editor of Business Standard on May 1, succeeding Shailesh Dobhal, who transitioned to a part-time role as Editorial Advisor. Dhoot previously served as Resident Editor in New Delhi and held senior roles at The Hindu and The Economic Times.

articles.headline: Public Sector Banks Prepare Capital Shore-up Ahead of ECL Framework
articles.summary: Several PSBs, including Bank of India and Indian Bank, are preparing to raise capital through equity and bonds to bolster CET1 buffers. The move anticipates the April 2027 introduction of the Expected Credit Loss (ECL) framework. Fundraising will also help lower government shareholding, which remains above 90% for some lenders.

articles.headline: Domestic Passenger Vehicle Wholesales Surge 27% in April
articles.summary: Domestic PV wholesales rose to approximately 450,000 units in April, supported by steady demand and previous tax cuts. Maruti Suzuki reported its highest-ever monthly domestic sales at 187,704 units, up 35.3%. Industry experts noted that rural penetration and a shift toward SUVs are driving the growth despite West Asia conflicts.

articles.headline: India's Fuel Exports Slump to Lowest Levels Since October 2022
articles.summary: Fuel exports fell to a 42-month low in April, averaging 947,000 barrels per day. The 18% decline was attributed to weak European demand and West Asia conflict disruptions. Reliance Industries saw a 16% drop in sales. Reduced exports may impact the national trade deficit as refiners prioritize domestic demand.

articles.headline: India Inc’s Large Foreign Acquisitions Deliver Underwhelming Returns
articles.summary: An analysis of the 10 largest overseas acquisitions by Indian firms shows 80% have underperformed the broader market. High debt outlays and sector downturns have resulted in returns on net worth remaining lower than pre-acquisition levels. Only Hindalco and Bharti Airtel emerged as market outperformers since their respective buys.

articles.headline: Expenditure Secretary Affirms Commitment to Rs 12 Trillion Capex Target
articles.summary: Expenditure Secretary V Vualnam acknowledged fiscal stress from West Asia energy disruptions but confirmed the government will preserve its Rs 12 trillion capex target for FY27. He noted that excise duty cuts on fuel could lead to a Rs 1.5 trillion loss, but the government remains committed to fiscal prudence and a deficit target of 4.3%.

articles.headline: ED Seeks Clarity on Sequencing of Money Laundering and Predicate Trials
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate has sought the Additional Solicitor General's view on whether PMLA trials should pause until predicate offense trials conclude. Director Rahul Navin noted that money laundering is often treated as a stand-alone offense globally. The ED currently maintains a 94% conviction rate with over 2,400 cases pending.

articles.headline: AGR Dues Cut to Provide Breathing Room for Vodafone Idea
articles.summary: The government's decision to cut Vodafone Idea’s AGR dues by 27% to Rs 64,046 crore is expected to help the telco raise bank debt and fund its Rs 45,000 crore capex plan. However, analysts warn that massive spectrum liabilities totaling Rs 1.25 trillion remain a major long-term challenge for the company's cash flow.

articles.headline: Passenger Electric Vehicle Sales Surged 70% in Early 2026
articles.summary: Passenger EV sales rose to 79,063 units in the January-April 2026 period. Mahindra & Mahindra recorded the highest growth at 170%, while Tata Motors maintained market leadership with 31,604 units. The data suggests a structural shift in consumer preference toward EVs despite the end of the financial year.

articles.headline: EV Startups Urge PMO to Relax PLI Eligibility Norms
articles.summary: Six EV startups, including Ather and River, have written to the PMO seeking a fresh application window and lower revenue thresholds for the auto PLI scheme. They claim the current structure creates a 16% cost disadvantage for emerging firms and that funds remain under-utilized by large, inactive beneficiaries.

articles.headline: JSW MG Motor India Reports 3% Growth in April Wholesales
articles.summary: JSW MG Motor India dispatched 6,018 units in April 2026, marking a 3% year-on-year increase. The growth was supported by steady momentum in both internal combustion and electric vehicle portfolios, despite supply chain disruptions and softer overall demand in the automotive market.

articles.headline: Vedanta Aims to Add Three Critical Minerals Within Five Years
articles.summary: Metals giant Vedanta Limited plans to add three new metals to its portfolio, evaluating potash, vanadium, and tungsten. Executive Director Arun Misra stated the company has secured several mineral blocks and is considering manufacturing rare-earth permanent magnets. Licensing for these strategic minerals has significantly accelerated.

articles.headline: Pharma Exports to US Slipped 10% in FY26 Despite Record Total
articles.summary: India's pharmaceutical exports reached a record $31.12 billion in FY26, but growth slowed to 2.13% as shipments to the US fell 10%. Generic price erosion and inventory corrections in the US market were offset by double-digit growth in emerging markets like Brazil, Nigeria, and Mexico. The industry expects 6-9% growth in FY27.

articles.headline: India Can Grow Above 7.5% Without Fueling Inflation: RBI Deputy Governor
articles.summary: RBI Deputy Governor Poonam Gupta stated that India has shown it can sustain growth rates above 7.5% while keeping inflation below 4%. She highlighted that the country's external account is supported by durable pillars like services exports and remittances rather than cyclical trends, and that monetary policy transmission is working effectively.

articles.headline: UPI Transaction Value Dips 1.7% in April from Record Highs
articles.summary: UPI payments fell to Rs 29.03 trillion in April, a 1.7% dip from March. Transaction volume also declined 1.3% to 22.35 billion. Experts attributed the slight drop to a high base in March, noting that daily transaction volumes actually rose from 730 million to 745 million as digital payments deepen in rural areas.

articles.headline: Coal India Production Falls 10% in April; Subsidiaries See Sharp Dips
articles.summary: Coal India reported a 9.7% decline in production to 56.1 million tonnes in April. Bharat Coking Coal recorded the steepest fall at 41.3%. Offtake also fell 2% to 63.2 million tonnes, forcing the company to rely on stockpiles. The drop is significant as summer electricity demand typically increases in the coming months.

articles.headline: MGNREGA Work Demand Plummeted 35% in April
articles.summary: Preliminary data shows just 13 million households demanded work under MGNREGA in April, a 35.3% year-on-year drop. Activists cited uncertainty regarding the transition to the new Viksit Bharat-Guarantee for Rozgar and Aajeevika Mission (VB-GRAMIN) as the cause. The government has allocated over Rs 1.25 trillion across both schemes.

articles.headline: Fintechs Accelerate RuPay Credit Card Rollout for UPI Spends
articles.summary: Fintech firms like Cred, PhonePe, and Google Pay are ramping up RuPay cobranded credit card launches to allow users to link credit to UPI. Cred reports that 20% of its scan-and-pay transactions are now through RuPay credit. The move aims to convert small-ticket debit spends into credit while tapping into new-to-credit users.

articles.headline: IOB Plans to Frontload Provisions for New ECL Norms
articles.summary: Indian Overseas Bank MD & CEO Ajay Kumar Srivastava stated the lender aims to address Expected Credit Loss (ECL) provisioning early, having already set aside Rs 1,750 crore. The bank reported 24% credit growth in FY26 and targets 12-13% growth for FY27, focusing on retail and MSME sectors while maintaining margins around 3.2%.

articles.headline: India’s Labour Market Facing Crisis of Job Precarity
articles.summary: PLFS data reveals that 60.7% of rural workers and 56.3% of urban workers in India lack written job contracts. Despite slight improvements, nearly half of all salaried employees are ineligible for paid leave or social security. Job insecurity remains highest in Bihar and Uttar Pradesh, often leading to industrial unrest in hubs like Noida.

articles.headline: Conflict Between IBC and Tax Laws Creates Uncertainty for Distressed Sales
articles.summary: A mismatch between insolvency and tax laws is leading to tax authorities denying the benefit of carrying forward past losses for resolved companies. Authorities argue they are not given proper hearings during resolution plan approvals. This legal gap is creating valuation uncertainty for bidders participating in stressed asset auctions.

articles.headline: Rahul Gandhi Slams Commercial LPG Price Hike as 'Election Bill'
articles.summary: Opposition leaders, including Rahul Gandhi and Akhilesh Yadav, criticized the record Rs 993 hike in commercial LPG prices. Gandhi described it as an 'election bill' and warned of future petrol and diesel hikes. Catering associations also protested the move, noting it forces them to absorb costs for contracts already finalized for the wedding season.

articles.headline: Delhi HC Stays Rs 450 Crore Anti-Profiteering Penalty Against Tata Play
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court stayed a GSTAT order requiring Tata Play to deposit Rs 450 crore for allegedly not passing on GST benefits. The court noted the GST rate on DTH services actually rose from 15% to 18%. Experts believe this signals that anti-profiteering claims must be based on firm evidence rather than mechanical assumptions.

articles.headline: Electronics MSMEs Expect Revenue Growth Moderation to 7-9% in FY27
articles.summary: MSMEs in the electronics sector are expected to see revenue growth slow slightly to 7-9% in FY27, despite support from the PLI scheme and semiconductor mission. Rupee depreciation and rising component costs due to the West Asia conflict are expected to squeeze margins by 50-100 basis points for these smaller manufacturers.

articles.headline: Transmission Bottlenecks Lead to 31 GW Renewable Energy Curtailment
articles.summary: India curtailed 31 GW of solar and wind capacity in the first quarter of 2026 due to grid constraints. Solar accounted for 27 GW of the losses, with Gujarat reporting the highest curtailment. Experts warned that without grid upgrades and battery storage investments, an increasing share of clean power remains unutilized.

articles.headline: Apple Forecasts Strong Q3 Growth Despite Memory Chip Crunch
articles.summary: Apple projected 14-17% revenue growth for the third quarter, beating analyst expectations. While the company warns of rising memory-chip costs and Mac computer shortages, incoming CEO John Ternus cited an 'incredible roadmap' ahead. Total sales reached $111.2 billion in the fiscal second quarter, driven by the iPhone 17 line.

articles.headline: Tehran Submits Proposal for Talks with US to Pakistani Mediators
articles.summary: Iran has sent a new proposal for negotiations with the United States to Pakistani mediators, potentially breaking the war impasse. While the US has not detailed private diplomatic talks, it recently imposed new sanctions on Iran-linked entities. Global oil prices fell slightly on hopes of a diplomatic breakthrough.

articles.headline: Trump Lifts Tariffs on British Whisky During King Charles' State Visit
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump announced the removal of tariffs on British whisky as a gesture of honor to King Charles. Trump praised the King during a four-day royal visit celebrating US independence. The move is seen as an effort to mend ties after Britain declined to join the US-Israeli military campaign against Iran.

articles.headline: Russian Filmmaker Loses Oscar Statuette at JFK Airport Security
articles.summary: Academy Award winner Pasha Talankin lost his Oscar statuette after JFK airport security refused to allow it on board a flight, labeling it a weapon. The 3.8kg award was checked as cargo on a Lufthansa flight but went missing before reaching Frankfurt. Talankin won the award for his documentary 'Mr. Nobody Against Putin'.

articles.headline: OpenAI CFO Reports Surge in Demand for Artificial Intelligence Products
articles.summary: OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar dismissed rumors that the company missed internal targets, citing a 'vertical wall of demand' for its products. Friar stated the startup is exceeding its high-level plans despite market volatility. The company recently faced scrutiny over its revenue and user growth forecasts for the coming periods.

articles.headline: SpaceX Spending on Starship Rocket Development Surpasses $15 Billion
articles.summary: SpaceX IPO registration documents reveal the company has spent over $15 billion developing the Starship rocket system. Elon Musk's reusable launch vehicle is central to ambitions for Moon and Mars missions and deploying Starlink satellites. The project's cost significantly dwarfs the development expense of the Falcon rocket.

articles.headline: Russia Successfully Test Launches New Soyuz-5 Rocket from Baikonur
articles.summary: Russia successfully launched its new Soyuz-5 rocket, featuring the world's most powerful liquid-fuel engine. The rocket can carry payloads up to 17 metric tonnes and is designed to significantly reduce launch costs for near-earth orbit objects like satellites. Roscosmos described the liftoff from Kazakhstan as flawless.

articles.headline: Huawei Expects 60% Revenue Jump from Artificial Intelligence Chips
articles.summary: Huawei projects its AI chip revenue will reach $12 billion this year, up from $7.5 billion in 2025. The 60% surge is driven by strong domestic demand from Chinese companies seeking alternatives to foreign technology. Orders have already hit $7.5 billion as Chinese firms shift toward homegrown offerings.

articles.headline: India Needs to Shift Strategic Focus to the Eastern Seaboard
articles.summary: Shekhar Gupta argues that India must look beyond threats from Pakistan and China to shore up maritime defenses in the east. He advocates for the urgent militarization of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, describing them as unsinkable aircraft carriers that can monitor the Malacca Strait and counter growing Chinese influence in Myanmar.

articles.headline: Iran Conflict Leaves India with Difficult Diplomatic and Security Choices
articles.summary: Shyam Saran warns that India's long-term interests require salvaging relations with Iran to avoid adversarial ties. He notes that Iran's capacity to disrupt the Strait of Hormuz affects India's energy security and that India has recently aligned too closely with the US-led security architecture in the Middle East at Iran's expense.

articles.headline: AI Productivity Surge Risks Creating a Zero-Demand Economy
articles.summary: Devangshu Datta discusses research showing that firms automating to achieve boundless productivity may inadvertently destroy consumer demand through massive layoffs. He explores potential policy interventions like automation taxes to counter a negative feedback loop where falling employment leads to a macroeconomic death spiral.

articles.headline: Pakistan’s Leadership Gains Centrality Through Regional Mediation Efforts
articles.summary: Mihir S Sharma argues that India's policy of isolating Pakistan has failed as Islamabad takes center stage in mediating between Iran and the US. He notes that Pakistan's military establishment has secured relative economic stability and repaired ties with Saudi Arabia and the US, demonstrating an ability to transcend diplomatic isolation.

articles.headline: Cash Market Turnover Rose 7% in April as F&O Dropped
articles.summary: Trading activity diverged in April, with cash turnover rising 7% to Rs 1.44 trillion while derivatives fell 6% following an STT hike. The BSE’s notional market share in derivatives crossed 50% for the first time. Analysts attributed the shift to market recovery and tighter regulations affecting algorithmic and retail traders.

articles.headline: Indian Stock Market Less Concentrated Than US Counterpart
articles.summary: India's stock market derives less of its total value from large firms than the US. India’s Herfindahl-Hirschman Index fell to 80.9 while the US index rose to 164, driven by the dominance of the 'Magnificent Seven' tech firms. India's market has diversified into financials and tech, aided by a record 103 IPOs in 2025.

articles.headline: Mutual Funds Turned Cautious in April Despite Stable Inflows
articles.summary: Mutual funds net equity purchases fell to Rs 26,000 crore in April from Rs 1 trillion in March. Fund managers adopted a cautious 'wait and watch' approach due to West Asia tensions and the new earnings season. Aggregate cash holdings of equity schemes fell to a 21-month low of 4.7% in March during the market correction.

articles.headline: Shriram Finance Targets 18% AUM Growth Supported by New Equity
articles.summary: Shriram Finance reported a 41% jump in Q4 net profit to Rs 3,010 crore. The company targets 18% growth, aided by MUFG Bank's equity stake acquisition. Despite minor asset quality changes, the lender expects steady margins and a decline in borrowing costs by 100 basis points over the next three years.

articles.headline: Max Healthcare Eyes FY27 Rebound with Massive Bed Expansion
articles.summary: Max Healthcare plans to add 1,500 beds in FY26 to drive volume growth. While Q4 revenue growth is expected to be moderate at 10.3%, brokerages remain positive on its maturing network and high margins. The hospital chain is focusing on brownfield expansions in Delhi and Mumbai to boost occupancy and ARPOB.

articles.headline: Investors Turn to NCDs as Middle Path Between FDs and Equities
articles.summary: Amid market volatility, investors are choosing Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs) for regular returns higher than fixed deposits. Experts advise sticking to AAA-rated secured issuers and matching NCD tenure to cash flow needs. Interest income is taxed at the investor’s slab rate, while long-term gains attract 12.5% tax.

articles.headline: New NPS Rules from July to Align Tier-II Account Charges
articles.summary: The PFRDA will align Tier-II account maintenance charges with Tier-I rates starting July 1. The move introduces uniform fees across sectors and formalizes the handling of dormant accounts. Each investment scheme within an NPS account could now attract its own maintenance charge, increasing costs for diversified investors.

articles.headline: Smallcap Index Outperforms Nifty 50 with 18% Monthly Rally
articles.summary: The Nifty Smallcap 100 index surged 18.4% in April, its best monthly performance since 2014. The rally, fueled by ceasefire hopes in West Asia, more than doubled Nifty 50's 7.5% gain. However, smallcap valuations are now higher than a year ago, trading at a 30.4x P/E multiple, raising risks of a correction if headwinds return.

articles.headline: Rising Popularity of AI Companions Raises Ethical and Safety Risks
articles.summary: Atanu Biswas examines the growing epidemic of loneliness driving people toward AI chatbots for romantic companionship. He cites tragic cases of suicides linked to these interactions and questions whether AI relationships can ever be genuine. He warns that AI's increasing emotional sensitivity could deepen human dependence on technology.

articles.headline: IndiGo Cuts International Seat Capacity by 17% Due to War Disruption
articles.summary: IndiGo is slashing its international flight capacity by 17% in May, making it the most affected non-Gulf carrier globally due to the Iran-US conflict. West Asian capacity has dropped 37%, while South Asia is down 9.3%. The airline expects a modest recovery in June assuming aviation fuel prices stabilize.

articles.headline: Navi Mumbai International Airport Dismisses IATA Concerns over High Fees
articles.summary: NMIAL rejected IATA's claim that high aeronautical charges will dampen demand, noting that airport fees are only 4-5% of airline operating costs. The airport argued that current tariffs are necessary for cost recovery and that any artificial reduction would only defer higher costs to future years.

articles.headline: IMD Forecasts Above-Normal Heatwaves and Rainfall for May
articles.summary: The IMD predicted above-normal heatwave days for Gujarat, Maharashtra, and the Himalayan foothills in May. However, total rainfall is expected to be more than 110% of the long-period average, potentially boosting kharif sowing. The southwest monsoon is likely to reach the Andaman and Nicobar islands by mid-May.

articles.headline: India's Shapewear Market Evolves into Versatile Fashion Essential
articles.summary: India's shapewear market is growing at 12-15% annually, shifting from concealed innerwear to fashion-forward wardrobe staples. Brands like Underneat and Krvvy are expanding into bralettes and bodysuits driven by body positivity and fabric innovation. Size inclusivity has become a key differentiator, with premium brands investing in extended sizing.

articles.headline: El Nino may impact crops until January 2027
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department warns that El Nino could last until January 2027, potentially affecting both kharif and rabi crops. Above-normal heatwaves are expected in several regions during May, although average rainfall for the month is likely to be above normal across most parts of the country.

articles.headline: BMW F 450 GS improves features but lacks distinctive character
articles.summary: The Made-in-India BMW F 450 GS replaces the G 310 GS, featuring a 420cc parallel-twin engine and advanced electronic assists. While offering premium build quality and nimble handling, reviewers noted the motorcycle's easy-ride clutch and lack of traditional GS ride quality may not meet all expectations.

articles.headline: Steep hike in commercial LPG prices; other fuels untouched
articles.summary: The government raised commercial LPG prices by ₹993 per 19-kg cylinder, a record 47.8% monthly increase. Prices for domestic LPG, petrol, and diesel remain unchanged to protect mass consumers, while bulk diesel and international jet fuel rates also saw upward revisions due to the West Asia crisis.

articles.headline: Capacity boost and tax relief drive April PV sales to record high
articles.summary: Domestic passenger vehicle sales grew 27% to 4.5 lakh units in April, reaching an all-time high. Manufacturers like Maruti Suzuki and Hyundai reported record monthly sales, citing expanded production capacities, income tax relief, and reduced interest rates as primary engines for the growth.

articles.headline: Government to maintain capex despite global challenges
articles.summary: Expenditure Secretary V Vualnam stated that the government remains committed to its budgeted ₹12.22 lakh crore capital expenditure despite fiscal stress from the West Asia crisis. Priority sectors like highways, railways, and urban development will continue to be preserved in the current fiscal.

articles.headline: Prepaid and postpaid ARPU gap nearly vanishes in telecom shift
articles.summary: The revenue gap between prepaid and postpaid mobile users has nearly closed, with prepaid ARPU reaching ₹194 against postpaid's ₹199. This shift suggests mass-market users now spend on par with premium subscribers, potentially reshaping future operator strategies and pricing.

articles.headline: Rural economy around Bhogapuram airport sees rapid transformation
articles.summary: The construction of Bhogapuram International Airport in Andhra Pradesh has triggered a surge in local business, employment, and real estate demand. Land prices have risen to ₹5 crore-₹8 crore per acre as hospitality chains and commercial developers invest in the airport ecosystem.

articles.headline: Empower India signs term sheet to buy UAE's MABIL
articles.summary: Sustainable energy solutions provider Empower India Ltd has signed a term sheet to acquire a majority stake in UAE-based MABIL. The cross-border transaction will be executed through a strategic share-swap arrangement to expand the company's international operations.

articles.headline: NFRA sets up four separate functional divisions for oversight
articles.summary: The National Financial Reporting Authority has established four distinct functional divisions to handle monitoring, investigation, and disciplinary proceedings. The move aims to streamline the regulator's operations and oversight of financial reporting standards.

articles.headline: UltraTech drives sharp turnaround at India Cements post-takeover
articles.summary: India Cements reported a net profit of ₹60 crore in Q4FY26 following its takeover by UltraTech. The turnaround was driven by brand integration, improved capacity utilization at old plants, and tighter cost controls, with UltraTech targeting significantly higher EBITDA by FY28.

articles.headline: Passenger vehicle registrations dip but electric vehicles show resilience
articles.summary: Overall passenger vehicle registrations fell 10.2% in April, but electric vehicle volumes remained steady, increasing their market share to a record 5.7%. Tata Motors led the EV segment with a 37.5% share, as consumers increasingly look to EVs as a hedge against fuel price volatility.

articles.headline: Skoda Volkswagen positions India as global engineering hub
articles.summary: The Skoda Volkswagen Group is expanding its R&D facility in Pune to serve as a lead development hub for global markets. The center will focus on software, digitalization, and future mobility programs, moving beyond local adaptation to original global product development.

articles.headline: Waaree Energies plans ₹30,000 crore capex for clean energy
articles.summary: Waaree Energies will invest ₹30,000 crore over the next 24 months to build a fully integrated clean energy platform. The expansion includes scaling solar cell and module capacity and entering the battery manufacturing segment with 16.5 GW of storage capacity by FY28.

articles.headline: Axis Energy plans ₹30,000 crore underground coal gasification project
articles.summary: Axis Energy Ventures has bagged two coal blocks in Odisha for producing gas through underground coal gasification. The company intends to invest ₹30,000 crore in the venture, converting coal into gas for power generation or ammonia production in deep mining zones.

articles.headline: Syngene sees pipeline traction despite 16% dip in Q4 profit
articles.summary: Syngene International reported a 16% year-on-year decline in profit to ₹153 crore for the March quarter, impacted by client-specific headwinds and higher operating costs. However, management signaled improving momentum in biologics and investments in new modalities like antibody-drug conjugates.

articles.headline: Food and hospitality sectors face pressure from LPG price hike
articles.summary: The third consecutive hike in commercial LPG prices is expected to drive up menu prices by 10-20% at restaurants. Industry bodies warn the record cost escalation may lead to business closures and job losses among smaller eateries and students who depend on affordable meals.

articles.headline: Government planning dedicated policy to push floating solar projects
articles.summary: The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy is developing a scheme to encourage large-scale floating solar projects across states. The initiative aims to utilize water bodies for solar installations to overcome land constraints and improve energy efficiency through the cooling effect of water.

articles.headline: Coal India April output falls 9.7% to 56 million tonnes
articles.summary: Coal India recorded a 9.7% year-on-year decline in production to 56.1 million tonnes in April. Industry observers attribute the drop to high pithead stocks, which currently stand at 125 million tonnes, impacting production schedules at five of the company's seven subsidiaries.

articles.headline: Maersk levies emergency freight surcharge on Gulf shipping routes
articles.summary: Global shipping major Maersk has introduced an emergency freight surcharge of up to $3,800 for cargo to and from the Gulf region. The move increases logistics costs for essential goods like pharmaceuticals and perishables which are being routed through alternative sea-land combinations.

articles.headline: Centre allows force majeure relief for government contracts
articles.summary: The Ministry of Finance has allowed government contractors to invoke force majeure due to supply chain disruptions linked to the West Asia war. Procurement entities may extend contract timelines by two to four months without penalties for impacted projects on a case-by-case basis.

articles.headline: MG Majestor evolution offers ruggedness but lacks Toyota's credibility
articles.summary: The MG Majestor, a replacement for the Gloster, features a 212-bhp twin-turbo diesel engine and advanced off-road capabilities. While offering a premium cabin and imposing road presence, it faces the challenge of matching the long-term credibility and resale value of the Toyota Fortuner.

articles.headline: Hyundai launches Ioniq 5 facelift in India at ₹55.7 lakh
articles.summary: Hyundai has updated its flagship electric SUV in India with a larger 84-kWh battery, increasing the claimed range to 690 km. The facelift includes subtle design tweaks, new alloy wheels, and enhanced technology features while maintaining its premium EV positioning.

articles.headline: Classic Legends launches BSA Scrambler 650 at ₹3.25 lakh
articles.summary: Classic Legends has expanded the BSA portfolio with the Scrambler 650, powered by a 652cc single-cylinder engine. The motorcycle is positioned as a road-biased scrambler with light off-road capability, featuring 187 mm of ground clearance and a 6-speed gearbox.

articles.headline: Morbidelli unveils N125V with liquid-cooled V-twin engine
articles.summary: Sino-Italian brand Morbidelli has revealed the N125V, a high-spec 125cc naked motorcycle featuring a unique V-twin engine. The bike includes premium hardware like USD forks, traction control, and dual-channel ABS, though it is currently not slated for launch in India.

articles.headline: Market turbulence hits AMC revenue and profits in March quarter
articles.summary: Volatile equity markets and geopolitical uncertainties led to a sequential decline in profitability for most Indian asset management companies in Q4FY26. While margins saw pressure, steady monthly SIP inflows above ₹30,000 crore helped maintain growth for larger fund houses.

articles.headline: RBI says 98.47% of ₹2,000 banknotes have been returned
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India announced that 98.47% of the ₹2,000 denomination banknotes in circulation have been returned as of May 2026. The total value of these notes in circulation has dropped to negligible levels from ₹3.56 lakh crore in May 2023.

articles.headline: Inward remittances unlikely to be hit by West Asia crisis
articles.summary: RBI Deputy Governor Poonam Gupta expressed confidence that India's inward remittances, currently exceeding $135 billion, will remain resilient despite the West Asia crisis. She noted that India's balance of payments is supported by structural strengths in services exports and FDI inflows.

articles.headline: PE-VC investments drop 32% in April amid geopolitical uncertainty
articles.summary: Private equity and venture capital investments in India fell 32% year-on-year to $1.9 billion in April. The decline in deal volume and value is attributed to investor caution regarding the West Asia conflict and a reduction in mega-deals compared to the previous quarter.

articles.headline: Exit polls may lift markets briefly but oil risks remain
articles.summary: Equity markets may see a short-term sentiment boost from state exit polls, but rising crude oil prices and domestic macro pressures remain the primary risks. Kotak Institutional Equities suggests that any rally may be capped as investors refocus on fuel pricing and economic fundamentals.

articles.headline: UPI daily volumes hit record high despite marginal monthly dip
articles.summary: UPI processed 22.35 billion transactions worth ₹29.03 lakh crore in April, showing a slight sequential dip from March's peak. However, average daily volumes reached a record 745 million, indicating that underlying user engagement remains strong in the digital payments ecosystem.

articles.headline: PhonePe's Share.Market appoints Vijay Ajmera to lead business
articles.summary: PhonePe’s broking arm, Share.Market, has named Vijay Ajmera as its new head following the departure of Ujjwal Jain. The leadership transition comes as the company sharpens its focus on the wealth and trading vertical while deferring its initial public offering plans.

articles.headline: Bombay HC sets aside ₹1,524 crore GST demand against Tata Sons
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court has overturned a ₹1,524 crore tax demand against Tata Sons, ruling that arbitration damages paid to NTT Docomo are not taxable under GST. The court held that compensation for breach of contract does not constitute a consideration for a taxable service.

articles.headline: Indian IT firms stick to cautious guidance amid weak demand
articles.summary: Tier-1 Indian IT companies including Infosys, Wipro, and HCLTech have provided conservative growth guidance for FY27. Management cited reduced discretionary spending by large global telecom and retail clients and continued uncertainty in European markets as primary factors.

articles.headline: White-collar hiring grew 6 per cent in April led by insurance
articles.summary: India's white-collar hiring saw a 6% year-on-year growth in April, primarily driven by the insurance, BPO, and real estate sectors. While telecom and banking saw declines, southern cities like Hyderabad and Chennai led the growth among major Indian metros.

articles.headline: Nielsen CFO emphasizes India's role as strategic innovation hub
articles.summary: Nielsen CFO Jessica Holscott stated that India serves as a key testing ground for the company’s streaming and advertising products. The firm plans to increase its Indian workforce and leverage local talent to drive innovations in content discovery and audience measurement.

articles.headline: Storage in over half of India's major reservoirs below 40%
articles.summary: Water storage levels in 166 major Indian reservoirs have dropped to 38.7% of total capacity. The southern region is most affected, with storage falling below 30% due to deficient pre-monsoon rainfall and heatwaves, raising concerns for agricultural and drinking water supplies.

articles.headline: Wheat procurement down 9% due to lower Madhya Pradesh buying
articles.summary: The government has procured 23.25 million tonnes of wheat as of April 30, a 9% decrease from last year. The decline is attributed to a 59% drop in purchases from Madhya Pradesh, although procurement in Haryana and Punjab remains ahead of or close to previous year levels.

articles.headline: UAE exits OPEC to prioritize national interests and production capacity
articles.summary: The UAE has officially left OPEC effective May 1, 2026, allowing it to move beyond cartel-mandated production quotas. The exit could benefit India by providing more flexible crude oil supply arrangements and potentially accelerating the use of local currencies for energy trade.

articles.headline: Agrizy opens phytochemistry R&D laboratory to target global wellness market
articles.summary: Agrizy has launched a new R&D laboratory focused on scientifically validated botanical ingredients. The facility aims to produce standardized herbal extracts like turmeric and ashwagandha to meet global regulatory standards for the $650 billion nutraceutical market.

articles.headline: Coconut oil and copra prices slump amid weak export demand
articles.summary: Coconut oil prices in Kerala have dropped significantly due to high production and export disruptions caused by the West Asia crisis. Traders report that a labor exodus and liquidated stocks in Tamil Nadu have further contributed to the downward price trend for fresh nuts.

articles.headline: Supply-side risks likely to support zinc prices despite volatile trade
articles.summary: Zinc prices are expected to find support from persistent input-cost pressures and supply disruptions linked to the West Asia conflict. However, analysts warn that continued weakness in China's property sector could weigh on steel consumption and dampen global demand for the metal.

articles.headline: Liquor revenues in GIFT City surge following eased access norms
articles.summary: Revenues from wine-and-dine facilities in GIFT City rose to ₹1.94 crore in FY26 as the Gujarat government eased prohibition norms. The revised framework allows visitors to access liquor with a photo ID, removing the previous requirement for specific consumption permits.

articles.headline: BIS formalizes new safety standards for disability mobility aids
articles.summary: The Bureau of Indian Standards has published six new standards for assistive products like crutches and portable wheelchair ramps. The move aligns the Indian disability-aid industry with international benchmarks to ensure safety and quality for essential mobility products.

articles.headline: Himadri Speciality Chemical aims to double profitability by FY28
articles.summary: Himadri Speciality Chemical announced a roadmap to double profits by FY28 through investments in LFP cathode active materials and specialty carbon black. The company aims to reduce India's import dependence while expanding its presence in the high-margin tyre and commercial vehicle segments.

articles.headline: Mindspace REIT CEO cites GCCs as key driver for office demand
articles.summary: Mindspace Business Parks REIT CEO Ramesh Nair stated that Global Capability Centres now account for half of the firm's portfolio. These centers offer stable income visibility through longer lease tenures and are less cyclical compared to traditional IT services companies.

articles.headline: Concorde leases 1.4 lakh sq ft office space to BHIVE Workspaces
articles.summary: Bengaluru-based Concorde has leased premium office space in Doddanekkundi to BHIVE Workspaces for an annual rental value of ₹16 crore. The 15-year lease will allow the co-working firm to add over 3,000 seats in the city's prominent east Bengaluru tech corridor.

articles.headline: High-level panel to help banks leverage capital for growth
articles.summary: Financial Services Secretary M Nagaraju said a new high-level committee will review public sector bank balance sheets to help them leverage capital more effectively. The panel will also focus on making the banking sector more inclusive and deepening the corporate bond market.

articles.headline: Karnataka Cabinet approves ₹228 crore KSDL production unit in Vijayapura
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has approved the establishment of a production unit for Karnataka Soaps and Detergents Ltd at a cost of ₹227.91 crore. The unit will help the iconic Mysore Sandal soap maker expand its market reach into neighboring states like Telangana and Maharashtra.

articles.headline: Karnataka allots 140 acres to US-based Applied Materials for ₹780 crore
articles.summary: The Karnataka Cabinet has approved the allocation of 140 acres near Kempegowda airport to US semiconductor equipment major Applied Materials Inc. The land, valued at ₹780 crore, is part of the state's push to attract advanced technology and semiconductor manufacturing industries.

articles.headline: US Pentagon reaches agreement with seven AI companies for network deployment
articles.summary: The US Department of Defense has signed agreements with seven firms, including OpenAI, NVIDIA, and Microsoft, to deploy AI capabilities on classified networks. The integration aims to streamline data synthesis and enhance decision-making in complex operational environments.

articles.headline: Aurobindo Pharma's CuraTeQ receives Health Canada nod for biosimilar
articles.summary: Aurobindo Pharma’s subsidiary CuraTeQ Biologics has received a compliance notice from Health Canada for its bevacizumab biosimilar. The drug, marketed as Bevqolva, is indicated for the treatment of various types of cancer, marking a key regulatory milestone for the company.

articles.headline: US Treasury warns shippers against paying Iranian tolls in Hormuz
articles.summary: The US Treasury has warned global shippers that paying tolls or making charitable donations to Iran for passage through the Strait of Hormuz risks punitive sanctions. The advisory clarifies that even indirect or disguised payments put companies at risk of legal action.

articles.headline: Pilots association claims airlines are penalizing crew for fatigue reports
articles.summary: The Airline Pilots’ Association of India has accused carriers of discouraging and penalizing pilots for filing fatigue reports. Data suggests a high rejection rate for these reports by major airlines, raising safety concerns following the recent deaths of two pilots due to health issues.

articles.headline: Star Health targets premium increase and higher profitability in FY27
articles.summary: Star Health and Allied Insurance plans to raise annual premiums by high single digits to beat inflation and improve its loss ratio. The company’s CFO stated that they are focusing on profitable business segments and tier-three city expansion rather than prioritizing market share over margins.

articles.headline: Trump plans 25% tariff on European Union car and truck imports
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump announced intentions to increase tariffs on EU-made vehicles to 25%, citing a failure to comply with trade deals. He clarified that vehicles produced within US plants would remain exempt from these new charges intended to encourage local production.

articles.headline: Major Political Development Rocks National Stage
articles.summary: A significant political event has occurred, drawing widespread attention and commentary from various national leaders. This development is expected to have far-reaching implications for current government policies and future elections, sparking intense debate among analysts and the public alike.

articles.headline: Economic Growth Faces Headwinds Amid Global Uncertainty
articles.summary: The latest economic report indicates that the nation's growth trajectory is encountering unexpected challenges, influenced by turbulent global markets and shifting trade dynamics. Experts are advising caution, recommending strategic fiscal adjustments to mitigate potential downturns.

articles.headline: Key Diplomatic Talks Conclude, Bilateral Ties Strengthened
articles.summary: High-level diplomatic discussions between India and a key international partner have successfully concluded, resulting in agreements aimed at enhancing bilateral cooperation across various sectors. Leaders expressed optimism for continued collaboration and regional stability.

articles.headline: Environmental Policy Under Review After Public Consultation
articles.summary: A crucial environmental policy is undergoing a comprehensive review following extensive public consultations and expert feedback. The proposed changes seek to address critical ecological concerns and promote sustainable practices across industries and communities nationwide.

articles.headline: Education Sector Initiatives Aim for Wider Access and Quality
articles.summary: New initiatives are being rolled out nationwide to significantly expand access to quality education, particularly in underserved regions. The government's plan includes curriculum reforms, teacher training programs, and technological integration to improve learning outcomes.

articles.headline: Infrastructure Project Gains Momentum in North
articles.summary: Several vital infrastructure projects across the country are reporting accelerated progress, with many nearing completion ahead of schedule. These developments are anticipated to boost regional connectivity, facilitate trade, and create numerous employment opportunities.
